posts.name: Kris_P@tten
posts.cooked: My son is a 2016 kiddo, what would you be buying if your kiddo was 2016 birth year?

posts.name: Marcus Dean
posts.cooked:
Double mag of canon and double mag of calon segur from BDX
16 piedmont is looking awesome
16 burgundy will have a heap of gems in red wine.
You have heaps of great choices

posts.name: Eric Egan
posts.cooked: Yep, lots of choice on 2016. We had our first child last year and I went long on Mas de Daumas Gassac, which made an excellent white as well as a great red for mid to long term drinking. I’ll also buy a fair bit of Bordeaux but am in no hurry on that front. I don’t expect prices will increase from release (and may in some cases drop), so will probably wait three or four years before I buy anything. In general though, I’m buying a lot of '16s from Languedoc; including a boatload of Faugeres and Terasses de Larzac.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked: Easy choice is Bordeaux. Generally considered a very good vintage and you have a wealth of producers with a track-record of going long. Montrose. Leoville Barton. Pichon Baron. Grand Puy Lacoste. Perhaps a First Growth or La Mish. Reads like Northern Rhone and Southern Rhone are strong as well.

posts.name: Gerhard P.  (P r @ € s e n t)
posts.cooked:
While Bordeaux is (most probably) fine - and Burgundy not bad either - the greatest success in France seems to be Rhone-valley: in CdP it´s definitely great, and in the North also really outstanding
(Hermitage, Cote-Rotie, Cornas …) - so lots of choices …
posts.display_username: Gerhard P.  (P r @ € s e n t)

posts.name: Ed Steinway
posts.cooked:
From the majority of the reviews both Pichon’s appear to be outstanding wines, if you are looking for Bordeaux.

Thanks,

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: If you want a sleeper grand cru red Burgundy that you could probably find in magnum, look for Chantal Remy’s Latricières-Chambertin. Not the other wines in the cellar, just the Latricières.

posts.name: Mark Ye
posts.cooked: Really nothing right now… since i doubt any of it is being sold, (unless it’s pre-arrival/EP type sales)?

posts.name: J Wei
posts.cooked: Read that 2016 Northern and Southern Rhone will be pretty fantastic as well.

posts.name: Kris_P@tten
posts.cooked:
Thanks everyone.

All good suggestion Alfert…will probably add Pontet Canet. Like the Pichon suggestions and had forgotten about GPL.

Gerhard, I plan on some CdP but 20 years is a long time for CdP, maybe Mordoree, Charvin and Beaucastel.

Northern Rhone for sure.

Be curious on some specific producers and wines from L-R Eric?

posts.name: Neil Gilmour
posts.cooked: I had 3 grand nephews/nieces born in 2017. If I buy something for them, what would hold up in their parents relatively warm houses (no wine cellars or basements)?

posts.name: james l moleberg
posts.cooked: Huet Vouvrays

posts.name: Kris_P@tten
posts.cooked:
james l moleberg:

Huet Vouvrays

Good shout, will grab some in next week.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked: Several threads on this concept, very cool you all are doing it. My son was born in 1998, never even thought of it. Not sure I could afford, then, what I would want to mature for him anyway - the Mouton bottle is killer. May go get it now. The problem is, he, like many kids his age, does not like wine. He would be much happier with a six-pack of January 2018 Natty Lite and a Cuban cigar. But, I do have 2 years more to groom him.

posts.name: David Glasser
posts.cooked:
Pichon Lalande and Conseillante in quantity.
Maybe a few bottles of first growths.

posts.name: Gerhard P.  (P r @ € s e n t)
posts.cooked:
Kris Patten:

Thanks everyone.

All good suggestion Alfert…will probably add Pontet Canet. Like the Pichon suggestions and had forgotten about GPL.

Gerhard, I plan on some CdP but 20 years is a long time for CdP, maybe Mordoree, Charvin and Beaucastel.

Northern Rhone for sure.

Be curious on some specific producers and wines from L-R Eric?

Kris,
For CdP in a fine year like 2016 20 years is nothing, if you stick to good (and more traditional) producers.

Sure Pegau, Beaucastel, but also Bois de Boursan ! (sold also as Jean-Paul VERSINO in Europe), usually a bargain, but will be released late this year or only in 2019 … great from casks …
Charvin, Marcoux, Les Cailloux (with “x”), Mont Olivet, Roger Sabon (Prestige), Vieux Telegraph, Domaine Ferrand, Bosquet des Papes, Lucien Barrot, Pierre Usseglio (Regular) … Vieux Donjon …

it doesn´t have to be Mordoree (which is fine) or the special cuvees - nor Rayas, Bonneau (probably not existing in 2016), Clos du Caillou (without “x”) Quartz or Reserve, Chapoutier (Barbe Rac, Croix de Bois) …
posts.display_username: Gerhard P.  (P r @ € s e n t)

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:

Northern Rhone for sure.

I have my short list of “buy every year” wines, but curious as to your buying interest.
